reachability_artifacts: built sworn notifications test generic-webhook invocation against a local HTTP fixture with captured body, headers, durable ID, result, and exit code
built sworn notifications test mobile invocation capturing the ntfy-compatible headers and a view-only board link
credential lifecycle capture proving notification configuration survives provider/account updates and logout without printing secret values

effort_complexity.rationale: See spec.json; implementer must confirm destination storage and URL policy against the shared credential authority before design review.

release_benefit: A SwornAgent operator can trust a recoverable autonomous release loop, receive durable attention pages, and monitor or safely control it from a mobile web board.

positive_scenarios: A signed generic webhook receives a stable event projection and durable identity.
A mobile push opens the matching read-only board state on a phone.
A temporary endpoint outage becomes a visible bounded retry and later delivery.
negative_scenarios: A notification cannot encode an operation mutation or provider callback action.
Redirects, malformed URLs, and unbounded responses fail closed into typed results.
Destination credentials and response bodies never reach logs or persisted events.
validation.benefit_hypothesis: Stable provider-neutral delivery plus a simple mobile adapter restores useful paging while keeping all mutations behind one authenticated command boundary.

design_decisions.choice: Keep a provider-neutral generic webhook and implement mobile push as an ntfy-compatible standard-library HTTP adapter.
design_decisions.rationale: The two transports exercise the same C-05 boundary without adding dependencies or provider-specific operation logic.

design_decisions.choice: Allow notification actions to open the board only, never to perform a mutation.
design_decisions.human_decision: Ratified 2026-07-14 (D4): notification actions may open the authenticated board, but mutations use the shared command service and local authorization boundary.
design_decisions.rationale: It prevents a delivery adapter from becoming a second authorization and command protocol.

design_decisions.choice: Disable redirects and return typed outcomes to the outbox instead of retrying inside adapters.
design_decisions.rationale: One retry authority preserves bounded behavior and avoids forwarding signed or secret-bearing requests to another host.
